popularStory.description: Maud or Matilda was the queen consort of King David I of Scotland whom she married following the death of her husband Simon. She was the great-niece of William the Conqueror and the granddaughter of E …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Matilda of Huntingdon於1074年7月2日出生在英國諾圖波藍德時，她的父親Waltheof II Earl of Northumbria29歲，母親Judith Of Lens20歲。她於1090年與Simon I de Senlis 1st Earl of Huntingdon-Northampton在英國亨廷格多史爾結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1130年4月23日在英國蘇格蘭培爾斯康享年55歲，埋葬在英國蘇格蘭培爾笛卡爾 斯康斯康 普利奧利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Matilda of Huntingdon于1074年7月2日出生在英国諾圖波藍德时，她的父亲Waltheof II Earl of Northumbria29岁，母亲Judith Of Lens20岁。她于1090年与Simon I de Senlis 1st Earl of Huntingdon-Northampton在英国亨廷格多史爾结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1130年4月23日在英国苏格兰培爾斯康享年55岁，埋葬在英国苏格兰培爾笛卡爾 斯康斯康 普利奧利。

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Huntingdon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Huntington
meanings: English (northern): habitational name from any of various places called Huntington, most of which are named with Old English huntena, the genitive plural form of hunta ‘hunter, huntsman’, + dūn ‘hill’ or tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Some, however, may have Old English hunting ‘hunting’ as the initial element, while others may derive from the Old English personal name Hunta (genitive Huntan), perhaps with Old English connective -ing-, + tūn. The surname may also arise from Huntingdon (Huntingdonshire), which derives from the Old English personal name Hunta (genitive Huntan) or Old English hunta (genitive huntan) + dūn.

History: A prominent American family of this name were founded by Simon Huntington, who himself never saw the New World, for he died in 1633 on the voyage to Boston, where his widow settled with her children. Their descendants include Jabez Huntington (1719–86), a wealthy West Indies trader, and Samuel Huntington (1731–96), who was one of the signers of the Declaration of Independence. Collis Potter Huntington (1821–1900) was an American railway magnate. Beginning with little education or money, he made a huge fortune, some of which he left to his nephew, Henry Huntington (1850–1927), who used the money to establish the Huntington library and art gallery in CA.

nameMeanings.name: Matilda
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

variants.name: Mathilda

The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When Matilda of Huntingdon was born on 2 July 1074, in Northumberland, England, her father, Waltheof II Earl of Northumbria, was 29 and her mother, Judith Of Lens, was 20. She married Simon I de Senlis 1st Earl of Huntingdon-Northampton in 1090, in Huntingdonshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 23 April 1130, in Scone, Perthshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 55, and was buried in Scone Priory, Old Scone, Perthshire, Scotland, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Matilda of Huntingdon est née le 2 juillet 1074, à Northumberland, Angleterre, son père, Waltheof II Earl of Northumbria, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Judith Of Lens, avait 20 ans. Elle a épousé Simon I de Senlis 1st Earl of Huntingdon-Northampton en 1090, à Huntingdonshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 23 avril 1130, à Scone, Perthshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 55 ans, et a été enterrée à Scone Priory, Old Scone, Perthshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Matilda of Huntingdon è nata il il 2 luglio 1074 nel Northumberland, England, sua padre, Waltheof II Earl of Northumbria, aveva 29 anni e sua madre, Judith Of Lens, aveva 20 anni. Lei sposato Simon I de Senlis 1st Earl of Huntingdon-Northampton nel 1090, nel Huntingdonshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 23 aprile 1130, nel Scone, Perthshire, Scozia, Regno Unito, all'età di 55 anni, e fu sepolta a Scone Priory, Old Scone, Perthshire, Scozia, Regno Unito.

ko.lifeSummary: Matilda of Huntingdon(이)가 1074년 7월 2일 영국 노르스벌랜드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Waltheof II Earl of Northumbria(은)는 29세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Judith Of Lens(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 1090년 영국 헌틴그도실르에서 Simon I de Senlis 1st Earl of Huntingdon-Northampton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1130년 4월 23일 영국 스코틀랜드 퍼스실르 스콘에서 55세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스코틀랜드 퍼스실르 드 스콘 스콘 플리오리에 묻혔다.

pt.2 July 1074–23 April 1130: 2 de julho de 1074–23 de abril de 1130
pt.Scone Abbey, Scone, Perth and Kinross, S: Scone Priory, Old Scone, Perthshire, Escócia, United Kingdom
pt.St Giles Hill, Winchester, Hampshire, En: Saint Giles Hill, Hampshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Matilda of Huntingdon nasceu em 2 de julho de 1074, em Northumberland, Inglaterra, seu pai, Waltheof II Earl of Northumbria, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Judith Of Lens, tinha 20 anos. Ela casou-se com Simon I de Senlis 1st Earl of Huntingdon-Northampton em 1090, em Huntingdonshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 23 de abril de 1130, em Scone, Perthshire, Escócia, United Kingdom, com 55 anos, e foi sepultada em Scone Priory, Old Scone, Perthshire, Escócia, United Kingdom.

ru.Scone Abbey, Scone, Perth and Kinross, S: скоун приори, олд скоун, пиртшир, Шотландия, Великобритания
ru.St Giles Hill, Winchester, Hampshire, En: сеинт джилесь хилл, хампшир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Matilda of Huntingdon родилась 2 июля 1074 г. в местности нортумбирланд, Англия, её отцу по имени Waltheof II Earl of Northumbria было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Judith Of Lens, 20. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Simon I de Senlis 1st Earl of Huntingdon-Northampton в 1090 г. в местности хантингдоншир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла 23 апреля 1130 г. в местности скоун, пиртшир, Шотландия, Великобритания в возрасте 55 лет и была похоронена в местности скоун приори, олд скоун, пиртшир, Шотландия, Великобритания.

es.St Giles Hill, Winchester, Hampshire, En: Saint Giles Hill, Hampshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Matilda of Huntingdon nació el 2 de julio de 1074, en Northumberland, Inglaterra, su papá, Waltheof II Earl of Northumbria, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Judith Of Lens, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Simon I de Senlis 1st Earl of Huntingdon-Northampton en 1090, en Huntingdonshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 23 de abril de 1130, en Scone, Perthshire, Escocia, Reino Unido, a la edad de 55 años, y fue sepultada en Scone Priory, Old Scone, Perthshire, Escocia, Reino Unido.
